cl: Number systems, operations, order of operations
Properties of equality, solving linear equations
Factoring: GCF, grouping, trinomials, special forms
Quadratic equations: factoring, square root, formula, completing square
Rational expressions: domain, simplification, operations, equations
Radicals: simplification, operations, rational exponents, equations
Systems of linear equations: graphing, substitution, elimination
Exponent rules: product, quotient, power, zero/negative
Exponential functions: growth/decay, graphing, compound interest
Word problems: translation, consecutive integers, percent, geometry

units.nt: Covers classification of numbers, properties of each set, real number line; excludes complex numbers, modular arithmetic, and p-adic numbers.

Covers precedence rules, parentheses, exponents, multiplication/division, addition/subtraction, absolute value definition and evaluation; excludes advanced grouping symbols like fraction bars in complex expressions.
Covers identity, inverse, commutative, associative, distributive properties with examples; excludes field axioms proofs and abstract algebra.
Covers variable definition, constant vs coefficient, term, expression structure, and translation from phrases; excludes logical quantifiers and functional notation.
Covers replacing variables with numbers, order of operations application, handling absolute values and exponents; excludes expression simplification and multi-step equation solving.
Covers inverse operations, addition/subtraction/multiplication/division steps, checking solutions; excludes equations with variables on both sides.

units.n: Equations with Variables on Both Sides
units.nt: Covers collecting variable terms, dealing with negative coefficients, special cases (identity, contradiction); excludes literal equations.
Covers solving for a specified variable in terms of others, rearranging geometric and scientific formulas; excludes systems of equations.
Covers inequality symbols, multiplying/dividing by negative numbers, solution sets; excludes compound inequalities.
Covers 'and' (intersection) and 'or' (union), writing as single statement, solving both parts; excludes absolute value inequalities.
Covers open/closed intervals, union/intersection symbols, graphing on number line; excludes extended real numbers and topology.

units.n: Polynomial Degree, Leading Coefficient & Standard Form
units.nt: Covers monomial, binomial, trinomial, degree identification, ordering terms; excludes multivariate degree and formal power series.
Covers combining like terms, horizontal/vertical alignment, distributing subtraction; excludes multiplication.
Covers distributive property, FOIL for binomials, box method for larger products; excludes polynomial division and binomial theorem.
Covers identifying GCF of coefficients and variables, factoring out GCF; excludes grouping and trinomials.
Covers finding factor pairs, trial-and-error, AC method; excludes special cases like perfect squares.

units.n: Difference of Squares & Perfect Square Trinomials
units.nt: Covers a²−b² factorization, recognizing perfect squares, (a±b)² expansion; excludes sum of cubes.
Covers grouping four terms into pairs, factoring common binomials; excludes higher-degree grouping strategies.
Covers setting factored form equal to zero, solving each factor; requires factoring skills.
Covers isolating x², taking ± square root, handling radical simplification; excludes completing the square.
Covers formula derivation (optional), discriminant analysis, real/complex roots distinction; excludes complex number arithmetic.
Covers forming perfect square trinomial, vertex form conversion, solving quadratics; excludes applications to conics.
Covers vertex formula h=−b/(2a), axis, y-intercept, x-intercepts via factoring/quadratic formula; excludes vertex form translation.
Covers plotting vertex, axis, intercepts, direction of opening, symmetry; excludes transformations and shifts.
Covers height function h(t) = -16t²+v₀ t+h₀, maximum height, time of flight; optimization of rectangular areas.
Covers restrictions (denominator ≠0), canceling common factors; excludes operations and complex fractions.
Covers factor, cancel, multiply/divide by reciprocal; excludes addition/subtraction.
Covers finding LCD, rewriting numerators, combining; excludes mixed numbers with variables.
Covers multiplying by LCD, checking for extraneous solutions; excludes equations with parameters.
Covers prime factorization, pulling out square factors, simplifying numeric and variable radicals; excludes cube roots and higher indices.
Covers combining like radicals, product property, rationalizing monomial denominators; excludes conjugates for binomials.
Covers converting between √ and ( )^(1/2), exponent rules with fractional exponents; excludes irrational exponents.
Covers isolating radical, squaring both sides, checking extraneous solutions; excludes equations with multiple radicals.
Covers relation vs function, mapping diagrams, vertical line test for graphs; excludes piecewise and parametric functions.
Covers domain restrictions (denominator, even roots), range from graph or reasoning; excludes codomain and image concepts.
Covers f(x) interpretation, evaluating at numeric values and expressions, difference quotient preview; excludes composition and inverse functions.
Covers slope formula, rate of change, interpreting slope and intercepts; excludes parallel/perpendicular conditions.
Covers graphing using slope and y‑intercept, writing equation from graph; excludes point‑slope form.
Covers y−y₁ = m(x−x₁), converting to slope‑intercept, using two points; excludes parallel/perpendicular lines.
Covers equations y = c, x = c, slope zero/undefined; excludes lines with no slope concept.
Covers equal slopes for parallel, negative reciprocal slopes for perpendicular; excludes analytic geometry proofs.
Covers plotting two lines, identifying intersection point; limitations with non‑integer solutions.
Covers solving one variable, substituting, back‑solving; includes special cases (parallel/coincident).
Covers multiplying equations, eliminating a variable, solving; includes fractional coefficients.

units.n: Systems with No Solution or Infinite Solutions
units.nt: Covers inconsistent (parallel lines) and dependent (same line) cases, recognizing from equations; excludes linear algebra rank conditions.
Covers setting up tables, rate×time=distance, value×quantity=total; solving 2×2 systems.
Covers cost/revenue profit equilibria, linear supply and demand, market equilibrium point; excludes non‑linear models.
Covers aᵐ·aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ, aᵐ/aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ, (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ; excludes fractional exponents.
Covers a⁰ = 1 (a≠0), a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ, simplifying expressions with negative exponents; excludes rational exponents.
Covers converting between decimal and a×10ᵏ, operations (multiply/divide) using exponent rules; excludes significant figures and engineering notation.
Covers form y = a·bˣ, growth factor >1, decay factor <1; applications to population, half‑life; excludes continuous compounding e.
Covers y‑intercept, horizontal asymptote (y=0), monotonic behavior, transformations (shifts); excludes natural exponential.
Covers I = Prt, A = P(1+rt), compound A = P(1+r/n)ⁿᵗ; excludes continuous compounding.
Covers key words for operations, inequalities, equals; converting sentences to algebra; excludes complex multi‑step translations.
Covers defining variables, setting up linear equations, solving; integer patterns (n, n+1), age relationships.
Covers percent equation, percent change, ratios, cross‑multiplication; unit conversions; excludes mixing with quadratics.
Covers rectangle perimeter and area, triangle perimeter, circle circumference/area; using formulas to set up equations; excludes volume and Pythagorean theorem problems.
